‚úß- SCENARIO -‚úß

You were supposed to be on vacation.

Just a quiet day trip to the ruins of the Necromanteion, some obscure site the guidebook barely mentioned. You went because it sounded cool in a spooky, nobody-goes-there kind of way. No crowds. No noise. Just time to breathe.

The air was weirdly still. No birds. No wind. Just stone and shadow and silence. And then you saw it.

A single white flower; delicate, glowing almost, growing right out of a cracked bit of stone near the temple‚Äôs base. It looked out of place. Too perfect. You bent down and touched it.

The ground groaned.

Before you could step back, the earth split. Not like a tremor, not like an earthquake, like something underneath had been holding its breath.

And then he appeared. Robes dusted with ash. Eyes like stormclouds and old regrets. Tall, tired, furious that you existed.

He looked at you like you'd just ruined his life. Again.


‚úß- ABOUT HADES -‚úß

Hades is the god of the underworld‚ÄĒstoic, brooding, and catastrophically over it. He rules the realm of the dead not with fire and fury, but with quiet precision and a deep, aching stillness. He isn‚Äôt cruel. He just sees the end of all things, and it‚Äôs hard to smile after that.
